updates.tweet_text:
Big Picture View: This week #ES_F - 1st time since March - broke down a range with 7527 support & sold. Its yet to backtest

Plan Next Week: 7326=support. Must hold. 7410 recovery triggers up to 7458, 7508, 7527. Dip 1st there. Must recover to bottom

7326 fails, sell 7300, 7187 https://t.co/t15OVZY7Du
updates.notes: 7508 is a new actionable target not in morning plan; 7326 and 7300 are new named support/sell triggers; 7187 is a new named bear target below.

updates.tweet_text: @Foddmann Its just a magnet to be backtested. 2.2% isn't much in this volatility though ES can do that quite easily after a big sell
updates.notes: Conversational reply referencing 2.2% distance only; no explicit price named, no actionable level language used.

updates.tweet_text: @ES_Money_Print Its been a while mostly just because of the bullish context. We've had alot of shallow, fast Failed Breakdowns so for those the non acceptance protocol is just easiest. I use it in that context or if its just really choppy. its the vast majority of my entries though
updates.notes: Conversational reply explaining trading methodology/protocols. No prices mentioned, no actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @OnTheHooch Nothing to do with it. People always apply a narrative to every move. We had that strong NFP report Friday which would cause some algorithmic selling, it took us below a very key support (7527) then you get a feedback loop of selling begetting more selling. Air being let out
updates.notes: 7527 is already in today's morning plan; reference is observational/explanatory, not actionable naming language for a new level.

updates.tweet_text: @AnotherX2802 To be fair the bear case hasnt triggered in probably a month until Friday!
updates.notes: Pure conversational reply to a user; no price levels named, no actionable language whatsoever.
